description: Redesign the quick-start onboarding checklist: reorder steps to follow a logical workflow (lot > locataire > prestataire > contrat > intervention > cloture), add tutorial/didactic content explaining why each step matters and how data connects, fix performance by moving data fetch to SSR.

userStories.title: SSR onboarding data fetch (performance fix)
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, when I load my dashboard, the onboarding checklist should appear immediately (no delay/flash) because the data is fetched server-side and streamed with the page.
acceptanceCriteria: getOnboardingProgress() called in AsyncDashboardContent server component
getSubscriptionStatus() called in AsyncDashboardContent server component
OnboardingProgress + subscription status passed as props through ManagerDashboardV2 to OnboardingChecklist
OnboardingChecklist no longer has useEffect fetching data (receives via props)
Checklist renders in initial HTML stream (no client-side flash)
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Moved onboarding queries to AsyncDashboardContent SSR. Subscription status check + 5 count queries run server-side. OnboardingChecklist receives data via props, no more useEffect fetch. localStorage dismiss check kept client-side.

userStories.title: New step order, queries, and types
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I see 6 steps in logical workflow order: (1) Creer un lot, (2) Ajouter un locataire, (3) Ajouter un prestataire, (4) Creer un contrat, (5) Creer une intervention, (6) Cloturer une intervention. Progress is tracked with correct DB queries.
acceptanceCriteria: OnboardingProgress interface updated: hasLot, hasAddedTenant, hasInvitedProvider, hasContract, hasIntervention, hasClosedIntervention
hasUploadedDocument removed from interface and query
New query: hasAddedTenant checks team_members with role='locataire'
New query: hasContract checks contracts table with team_id
STEPS array reordered: lot > locataire > prestataire > contrat > intervention > cloture
All 6 queries run in parallel via Promise.all
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Updated OnboardingProgress interface (added hasAddedTenant + hasContract, removed hasUploadedDocument). Updated server action + SSR queries to 6 parallel checks. STEPS reordered: lot > locataire > prestataire > contrat > intervention > cloture. URLs now point directly to creation pages.

userStories.title: Tutorial/didactic content per step
userStories.description: As a new gestionnaire, each onboarding step shows educational content explaining: (a) what the entity is, (b) why it matters, (c) how the data will be used later in the app. This helps me understand the platform while setting it up.
acceptanceCriteria: Each step has a 'whyItMatters' text (1-2 sentences, benefit-oriented)
Each step has a 'howItConnects' text (1 sentence, explains downstream usage)
Educational content visible below each pending step (not just on hover)
Completed steps show only label (educational text hidden to save space)
Content is in French, professional tone, outcome-focused (not feature-focused)
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Added whyItMatters + howItConnects fields to ChecklistStep. Tutorial content shown for current step via border-l + lightbulb icon. Benefit-oriented French text for all 6 steps. Contract step: 'Le contrat lie un locataire a un lot' (user requirement). Also started US-004 visual improvements (step numbers, current step highlight) in same change.

userStories.title: Sequential flow UX + visual improvements
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I see a visual progression through the steps: the current step is highlighted, future steps are visually muted (not locked, but clearly suggesting order), and completing a step triggers a satisfying transition.
acceptanceCriteria: Current (first incomplete) step has distinct visual treatment (primary border/bg, slightly larger)
Future steps are visually muted (lighter text, smaller icons) but still clickable
Completed steps compact with green checkmark + strikethrough (existing behavior preserved)
Step numbers visible (1-6) to reinforce sequential flow
Smooth transition when step completes (animate from current to next)
Progress bar shows X/6 with percentage
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Most visual improvements implemented in US-003: step numbers (1-6 replacing icons for incomplete steps), current step primary highlight (bg-primary/5, border-primary/20, font-semibold text-primary), future steps muted but clickable, icon shown on right of current step. CSS transition-all handles smooth state changes on re-render.
